_registration: VERDICT 078 fixtures — idea-engine PROPOSAL 065 'outbox rollover stub saturation' (ideas/fleet/outbox-rollover-stub-saturation-2026-07-15.md @ idea-engine main 85114c5b6168e9ca5012ca35ad78556b4e56bf32, landed via idea-engine PR #428, head-ref f9f328d). Every model constant below is copied verbatim from the PROPOSAL 065 outbox block / idea doc (measured-then-pinned there; where the doc and the outbox block disagree, the block wins — no disagreement found; ONE internal inconsistency inside the shared F3 anchor text is recorded under anchors.first_spacing_formula_as_written and handled as anomaly A1, value gated, expression reported). Fully hermetic: the runner reads ONLY this file; zero repo/network reads at verdict time (the P017-P064 hermetic precedent). This file is committed BEFORE the runner exists (the V072-V077 registration discipline; no fix-forwards — the first complete run of the registered pipeline is the accepted run).

world.composition: live file = header H0 + roll receipts + pointer stubs + full blocks, all integer bytes
world.T_note: the committed '200KB' read as KiB; the 200,000-decimal reading lands N* = 224 — immaterial to every band, disclosed, reported as a reporting row
world.s_note: stub grammar grid: 265 ~ heading+target only, 530 = the committed heading+target+idea-URL (measured mean 529.9, min 517, max 543), 1060 ~ summary-quoting stub
world.h_r_note: roll receipt appended to the live file at every roll (measured ROLLOVER 001 = 1,002 B; the committed shape — the receipt lives in the live outbox)
world.W_note: newest W blocks are non-terminal (verdict pending) and stay full through a roll ('terminal-blocks-only'); roll 1 kept exactly one; ORDER 003 >=1-in-flight + WIP-3 cap bound normal operation 1-3; 8 = verdict-lag stress

policies.P-STUB: committed — one pointer stub (s bytes) per evicted block; stubs and receipts both permanent
policies.P-RANGE: one range stub (s bytes) per roll; receipts permanent
policies.P-COMPACT: one range stub per roll AND the receipt chain collapsed to the newest receipt — post-roll non-window mass is H0 + h_r + s exactly

world.process: proposals append one full block each; a roll fires at the first append that makes the file >= T; the roll evicts every terminal full block (all but the newest W; evict count = max(0, fulls - W)), appends its receipt, and leaves tombstones per policy
world.saturation: post-roll size >= T; N* = total proposals appended at saturation
world.spacing_definition: spacing_j = appends between roll j-1 and roll j (spacing_1 = appends from the empty file to roll 1); Delta_k = ceil((T - F_k)/b) with F_k the post-roll-k size — the recurrence Arm A iterates and the event walk (Arm B) must re-derive

alternate_timing_schedule.rule: roll every 25 appends regardless of size
alternate_timing_schedule.gate: F2a — same post-roll size at the same terminal set: post-roll size == H0 + k*h_r + s*(25k - W) + W*b (P-STUB form; P-RANGE s*k; P-COMPACT s + h_r) at every alternate-schedule roll, exact

metrics.floor_series: F_k = post-roll live-file size after roll k, exact integer
metrics.spacing_series: Delta series with the collapse index = first roll whose spacing <= 2
metrics.roll_count: K = index of the saturating roll
metrics.wall_table: N*(policy, T, s, b, W) over the full 3x3x3x3 grid, all three policies (non-saturating cells reported as unbounded with the constant floor)
metrics.range_multiplier: N*_range / N*_stub at the decision cell, exact rational
metrics.wall_composition: stub mass / receipt mass / window mass / header at saturation, exact integers summing to the saturation floor
metrics.timing_invariance: F2a check under the alternate schedule
metrics.wall_date: REPORTING-ONLY arithmetic at the observed pace (see reporting_rows)

REJECT.compact_clause: P-COMPACT holds a CONSTANT floor over 100,000 appends

decision_rule.INVALID: any F1-F6 gate failure (controls misbehaving — report, no ruling)

APPROVE.first_spacings: every one of the first 20 spacings >= 4
APPROVE.note: mutually exclusive with REJECT via 300 vs 500 on the same exact integer

NULL_axes: band-straddle (N*_stub in (300, 500), or the thrash clause alone fails, or the multiplier clause alone fails — the finding is the exact floor/spacing curves and the wall table)
an invariance-theorem failure without gate failure (the drafter's receipt algebra is wrong — the corrected law is the finding)
P-COMPACT saturating (the corrected boundedness condition is the finding)
twin-arm disagreement surviving the INVALID diagnosis

gates.F1: model & mechanism identities — conservation (proposals appended = stubbed + archived-window + live full blocks at every step, per policy semantics); the spacing identity Delta_k = ceil((T - F_k)/b) re-derived by the literal event walk; the floor law F_k = H0 + k*h_r + s*n_k + sum(newest W blocks) at EVERY roll under P-STUB (P-RANGE form s*k; P-COMPACT form s + h_r constant); file size == sum of parts, exact, at every append
gates.F2: the three structure theorems, exact — (a) FLOOR LAW timing invariance: the alternate pinned schedule (roll every 25 appends regardless of size) yields the SAME post-roll size at the same terminal set, every comparison exact; (b) RECEIPT-FREE INVARIANCE: at h_r = 0, W = 0 the wall equals ceil((T - H0)/s) = 386 and is IDENTICAL across the whole b grid {8000, 16000, 24000}; (c) COMPACT BOUNDEDNESS: under P-COMPACT the floor is the constant H0 + h_r + s + W*b = 34,030 and the spacing is constant 11, held over 100,000 appends; plus MONOTONE THRASH: F_k strictly increasing and Delta_k non-increasing under P-STUB at every grid cell
gates.F3: closed-form anchors (decision cell) — N*_stub = 233 with K = 51 rolls; first spacing 13 (see anchors.first_spacing_formula_as_written — value gated, the as-written expression is anomaly A1); first floor 39,330 = 500 + 1000 + 530*11 + 32000; collapse index 34 (rolls 34-51 all have spacing <= 2, count 18); saturation floor 205,930 with exact wall composition stub 122,430 / receipts 51,000 / window 32,000 / header 500; N*_range = 671 (multiplier 671/233 ~ 2.88); P-COMPACT floor 34,030
gates.F5: degeneracies — s = b = 16000 (stubs as big as blocks): N* = 13 at the decision cell's other constants; s = 0 AND h_r = 0 (true deletion): no saturation in 100,000 appends, floor constant H0 + W*b = 32,500
gates.F6: battery — Arm B reproduces every Arm-A published number exactly (every wall, every floor entry, every spacing entry, every composition row); twin independently-written decision evaluators agree on the ruling token; Arm-R draw-count sentinels (1 uniform per appended proposal) counted and asserted; aux seed 20261570 never read (constructor registry); stdout + results.json byte-identical across two process runs; CPython minor pinned (3.11) and asserted

anchors.first_spacing_formula_as_written: the proposal/idea file writes 'first spacing 13 = ceil((204800 - 32500)/16000)' — the as-written expression evaluates to 11, NOT 13; the event-walk-correct expression is ceil((204800 - 500)/16000) = 13 (spacing_1 runs from the empty file, header only — the hand world's own first spacing 5 = ceil((100 - 10)/20) confirms the convention); the anchor VALUE 13 is gated, the expression slip is anomaly A1, ruling-neutral

arms.A: DECISION arm, seedless — the exact integer recurrence (Delta_k = ceil((T - F_k)/b) via -(-x//y), floor update per policy, evict = max(0, fulls - W)) over the full grid; byte-identical across process runs, stdlib only
arms.B: twin, seedless, INDEPENDENTLY-WRITTEN — a literal byte-ledger simulator: an actual list of (kind, size, terminal-state) records appended, scanned, and rolled; file size recomputed as sum of record sizes at EVERY append (F1); must reproduce every Arm-A published number EXACTLY, including every floor and spacing entry; powers the second decision evaluator
arms.R: seeded, REPORTING-ONLY — block sizes drawn per proposal from the pinned mix {8000: 1/4, 16000: 1/2, 24000: 1/4} (mean exactly 16000) via exact-rational comparison on one uniform per appended proposal; 10,000 process replications on random.Random(20261567) at the decision cell's other constants under P-STUB, reporting the wall DISTRIBUTION beside the deterministic wall; stability leg random.Random(20261568) at 2,000; presentation shuffle 20261569 (read by the presentation-shuffled stdout wall-table listing only); NO statistical gate rides Arm R — a mix-induced drift from the deterministic wall is a named finding, never a ruling

arm_R_params.draws_per_episode: 1 per appended proposal (exactly N* draws per episode, counted and asserted)

seeds.note: 20261567-570 pinned by the P065 drafter, strictly above the tree high-water 20261566 (P064's registration) and the sim-lab genuine high-water 20261562 (V074's block); this verdict session allocates NO seeds of its own — the registered seeds are the contract (the V077 precedent: the drafter's allocation IS the session's seed set). Registry high-water after this slice: 20261570 (reserved aux; highest READ 20261569).

drafter_disclosed_never_gated.note: the drafting prototype's disclosed landing beyond the F3-gated anchors, re-derived from scratch this session and COMPARED, never gated; any mismatch is a named finding

retrodiction.gap_note: seven resident fulls + ~9.5 KB un-modeled ASK/ACK/CLOSE-OUT blocks account for the gap, disclosed F1 boundary, REJECT-ward only

fixture_level_conventions.C1: size-mix draw (Arm R): u = rng.random(); size = 8000 iff Fraction(u) < 1/4 strict, 16000 iff Fraction(u) < 3/4 strict, else 24000 (exact-rational comparison, no float thresholds); exactly one uniform per appended proposal
fixture_level_conventions.C2: Arm-R episodes run the SAME event semantics as the deterministic arms with realized (variable) block sizes: floor law per realized sizes, roll at first append making file >= T, saturation = post-roll >= T; per-episode draw count == that episode's N*, asserted
fixture_level_conventions.C3: unbounded cells: Arm A walks the full 100,000 appends; the published record for a non-saturating cell is {'unbounded', constant floor, constant spacing, walk length}; Arm B twins the decision-cell P-COMPACT and true-deletion walks at full 100,000-append length and every other non-saturating cell on a 5,000-append prefix that must match Arm A's floors/spacings exactly (the published prefix)
fixture_level_conventions.C4: published exact surface scope: full wall table (3 policies x 81 cells), decision-cell floor + spacing series in full, composition rows for every saturating P-STUB/P-RANGE cell at the decision T/s/b/W margins, hand world, degeneracies, theorem checks; every published number integer or exact rational (num/den strings)
fixture_level_conventions.C5: canonical serialization: exact rationals as reduced num/den decimal strings, floats via repr(), json sort_keys = True, no timestamps in results.json or stdout
fixture_level_conventions.C6: the wall-date reporting row uses exact Fraction day arithmetic: days_remaining = (N*_stub - pipeline_at) / (intervals/days), date = reference_date + ceil(days_remaining) calendar days, reported beside the drafter's ~2026-07-27; reporting-only
fixture_level_conventions.C7: twin decision evaluators: evaluator A reads Arm-A numbers, evaluator B reads Arm-B numbers, independently-coded band logic, registered order REJECT -> INVALID -> APPROVE -> NULL; INVALID short-circuits on any gate failure
fixture_level_conventions.C8: the aux-seed assertion is enforced by a constructor registry: every random.Random construction logs its seed; the registry must equal [20261567, 20261568, 20261569] exactly and never contain 20261570
fixture_level_conventions.C9: the F2a timing-invariance closed forms: P-STUB post-roll size H0 + k*h_r + s*(25k - W) + W*b; P-RANGE H0 + k*h_r + s*k + W*b; P-COMPACT H0 + h_r + s + W*b (k >= 1); checked at the decision cell for 1,000 alternate-schedule appends AND cross-checked against the threshold-schedule floor law at every threshold roll (same closed form in (k, appends))
fixture_level_conventions.C10: conservation (F1) tokens per policy: P-STUB appended == stubbed + live fulls; P-RANGE/P-COMPACT appended == archived (evicted) + live fulls, with stub records counting rolls not blocks — the per-policy identity is asserted at every roll
fixture_level_conventions.C11: anomaly convention: drafter-disclosed constants re-derived and compared line-by-line; every mismatch is a first-class named anomaly in REPORT.md (the V077 A1 precedent); the F3 first-spacing expression slip is pre-identified as A1 in anchors.first_spacing_formula_as_written and asserted in-sim (as-written expression == 11 != 13 == event walk)

runtime_pins.determinism: stdout + results.json byte-identical across two full process runs (external diff + sha256, hashes in REPORT.md)
